definition_origin: The persistent CTA pattern (always-visible upgrade button) was popularized in mobile freemium apps 2018-2022 by Spotify (Spotify Premium 'Try Premium' floating prompt), Notion ('Upgrade' top-right persistent), and Duolingo ('Try Super' on practice screens). The 10-20% revenue lift figure is sourced from the PDF Deep Dive Into App Onboarding (citation 67) and aligns with RevenueCat 2024-2025 paywall-trigger benchmarks: feature-gated and persistent CTA placements together account for 45-55% of all post-onboarding conversions. The pattern is the canonical alternative to hard-paywall-only monetization for freemium B2B (Linear, Notion, Figma) and freemium consumer (Spotify, Duolingo, YouTube Music).
core_mechanism: Three mechanisms. (1) Availability bias — persistent CTA keeps the upgrade option in working memory across all interactions; users facing a feature limit don't have to remember a paywall pathway, it's already visible. (2) Reduced friction at peak intent — when a user hits a feature wall (download limit, save limit, AI-credit cap), the upgrade is one tap away, not buried in Settings → Account → Plan → Upgrade. (3) Social-proof / status visualization — a persistent 'Upgrade to Pro' badge implicitly markets premium continuously; free users see the Pro affordance in their UI map every session. The 10-20% revenue lift comes primarily from reducing the lost-funnel cost of high-intent free users who can't easily find the paywall when they hit feature limits.
neuroscience_correlate: Working memory (Baddeley) — persistent CTA reduces cognitive load of recalling upgrade pathway. Approach motivation when user hits a feature limit (frustration → mesolimbic response) is captured immediately by adjacent CTA without prefrontal deliberation about how to upgrade. Selective attention (parietal cortex) — initial training to ignore the CTA after repeated exposure is the failure mode (banner blindness).
empirical_evidence: PDF Deep Dive Into App Onboarding (citation 67): 'Persistent CTA throughout interface = 10-20% revenue lift.' RevenueCat 2024-2025 cross-app paywall-trigger analysis: feature-gated + persistent CTA placements account for 45-55% of post-onboarding conversions, vs ~10% for purely re-engagement-driven re-paywall views. Adapty 2026: persistent CTA paywall-view rate is ~3-5% per session (vs 100% for hard onboarding paywall) but cumulative paywall views over 30 days for free users is 8-12x higher than single hard paywall. Spotify 2023 product blog: 'Try Premium' button on Now Playing screen drives 22% of mobile Premium conversions. Duolingo 2024 investor reporting: persistent 'Try Super' impressions correlate with 18% of Super conversions. Mobbin / Layer / B2B SaaS teardowns 2024-2025: Linear, Notion, Figma all use persistent top-right Upgrade CTA pattern; Linear's blog cites 'visible plan affordance' as part of design philosophy.
measurable_outcome_metric: persistent_cta_impressions, persistent_cta_clickthrough_rate (CTR), persistent_cta_to_paywall_view, persistent_cta_to_purchase, banner_blindness_rate (impressions per click over 30d), free-user 30d revenue per persistent_cta_view.
conversion_lift_benchmark: +10-20% revenue lift (PDF Deep Dive Into App Onboarding, citation 67). +22% of Spotify mobile Premium conversions from Now Playing 'Try Premium' button (Spotify 2023). +18% Duolingo Super conversions from persistent 'Try Super' (Duolingo 2024 investor reporting).
engineering_tactics: Pattern A — Top-bar (B2B / productivity): persistent 'Upgrade' button top-right of every authenticated screen; opens paywall sheet. Implementation: <Header right={<UpgradeButton onPress={() => navigation.navigate('paywall', { source: 'top_bar' })} />} />. Pattern B — Floating action button (consumer / wellness): bottom-right floating 'Try Pro' chip on home screen + content screens; suppressed on player/reader/full-screen contexts. Pattern C — Tab-bar embedded (consumer): 'Pro' tab in tab-bar; tap routes to feature-comparison + paywall. Pattern D — Inline-feature gated (hybrid): persistent 'Pro' badge on locked feature affordance (download, AI generation, advanced filter); tap opens contextualized paywall. CRITICAL: rotate creative every 14 days (banner blindness) — cycle copy ('Try Pro', 'Unlock everything', '50% off this week'). Suppress for paying users (check customer_info.entitlements.active.pro before render). Telemetry: emit persistent_cta_viewed (placement, screen, copy_variant), persistent_cta_clicked (same), persistent_cta_to_purchase (correlate via 30d window). A/B variant via PostHog feature flag. Frequency cap: max 1 persistent CTA per screen (do not stack top-bar + FAB + tab — Decision Paralysis antipattern).
real_world_examples: 1) Spotify Free — Now Playing screen has 'Try Premium' chip below player controls; +22% of mobile Premium conversions per Spotify 2023 product blog. 2) Notion Free — top-right 'Upgrade' button on every workspace screen; routes to plan-comparison page. 3) Linear Free — top-right 'Upgrade' on every project screen; cohorts with hit-feature-limit drive most conversion. 4) Duolingo — persistent 'Try Super' card on home screen + practice mode; 18% of Super conversions per investor data. 5) YouTube Music Free — 'Upgrade to Premium' top-right + ad-skip prompts (hybrid persistent + interstitial). 6) Figma Free — 'Upgrade' top-right when team-feature limit hit (contextual persistent). 7) Headspace Free — limited library + 'Try Plus' top-bar on locked content. 8) Calm Free — 'Try Calm Premium' inline on locked sleep stories (Pattern D contextual).
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile: limited screen real estate — top-bar + FAB compete; tab-bar embedded is best fit. Web: more screen real-estate, top-right Upgrade is standard B2B SaaS pattern (Notion, Linear, Figma all use). Mobile tap-target ≥44x44pt (Apple) / 48x48dp (Material) — FAB minimum. Web cursor-hover affordances allow more subtle persistent CTAs (color-shift on hover); mobile must be visually obvious (filled button or chip).
platform_design_language_alignment: Liquid Glass (iOS 26+): translucent FAB with bold left-aligned 'Try Pro' text + dynamic morph on tap. M3 Expressive (Android 16+): expressive shape system FAB with M3E motion (bouncy scale on press); 4x faster element discovery (18,000 study) makes persistent CTA easier to find. iOS HIG: tab-bar 'Pro' tab is acceptable; floating overlays should not obscure primary content. Material 3 FAB guidelines: extended FAB with text label preferred for upgrade (not icon-only).
category_fit: STRONG: freemium B2B (Notion, Linear, Figma), freemium consumer (Spotify, Duolingo, YouTube Music), wellness freemium (Calm, Headspace free tier), AI-wrapper with usage caps (ChatGPT Free → Plus), social with limits (Tinder Free → Gold). WEAK / EXCLUDE: hard-paywall-only apps (Cal AI, Prayer Lock — there's no free user to upsell), kids/family apps (FTC + COPPA discourage continuous monetization to minors), one-time-purchase apps (no recurring upgrade path).
solo_dev_feasibility: same_day. Implementation: add persistent button to header/tab/FAB component, wire to paywall navigation, gate render on customer_info.entitlements.active. Paywall already exists (item 34). Add A/B variant via PostHog flag in 1-2 hours. Banner-blindness creative rotation requires 2-3 copy variants in JSON config (5-min change).
tooling_options: RevenueCat customer_info hook (entitlement gating). Adapty profile.accessLevels (entitlement gating). PostHog feature flags (variant assignment). React Native Reanimated 3 (FAB animation). Native iOS UIBarButtonItem / Android MaterialButton (top-bar). React Navigation Header (cross-platform). Superwall paywall trigger from persistent CTA (campaign rule: trigger=persistent_cta_clicked).
claude_design_application: Prompt fragment 1: 'Add a persistent Upgrade CTA to the React Native authenticated app shell. Pattern: top-right header button labeled Try Pro on B2B screens, floating bottom-right chip Try Pro on consumer screens; suppressed on full-screen player/reader/onboarding contexts. Gate render on RevenueCat customerInfo.entitlements.active.pro (hide for paying users). Wire to paywall navigation with source param: navigation.navigate("paywall", { source: "persistent_cta", placement: screen_name }). Rotate copy every 14 days via PostHog feature flag (variant A: Try Pro, B: Unlock everything, C: -50% this week). Forbid: stacking multiple persistent CTAs on same screen (Decision Paralysis), showing on onboarding/paywall/player screens, indigo-500 button, fake urgency.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Generate persistent CTA telemetry: emit persistent_cta_viewed (placement, screen, copy_variant) on render, persistent_cta_clicked on press, persistent_cta_to_purchase via 30d attribution window. Build PostHog dashboard: CTR by placement, conversion by copy_variant, banner_blindness_rate (impressions/click over 30d).'
stitch_application: Prompt fragment 1: 'Generate the persistent Upgrade CTA chip for a wellness app home screen. Position: bottom-right, 16pt margin from edges, above tab-bar. Style: filled chip, translucent material (Liquid Glass), Cabinet Grotesk 14pt: Try Calm Premium. Icon: subtle sparkle 16pt left of text. Pressed state: 0.95 scale + opacity 0.8. Forbid: indigo-500, gradient, fake countdown badge. Forbid stacking with FAB or tab-bar Pro tab — single persistent CTA per screen.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Generate the B2B top-right Upgrade button for a Linear-style productivity app. Position: header top-right, 16pt from right edge. Style: outlined button, Söhne 14pt Upgrade, hover-state filled (web only). Suppressed on Settings/Account screens (avoid double-CTA). Cabinet Grotesk for product wordmark left, Söhne for nav.'
ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI defaults: (1) Stacks multiple CTAs (top-bar + FAB + tab-bar Pro tab) — Decision Paralysis antipattern. (2) Renders for paying users (forgot to gate on entitlement). (3) Hardcoded copy 'Upgrade' — never rotates, banner blindness within 14 days. (4) Indigo-500 + Lucide upgrade-arrow icon. (5) Persistent CTA on player/reader/onboarding screens — interruptive. (6) Fake urgency badge ('Sale ends in 9:59'). Counter-prompt: 'Single persistent CTA per screen (never stack). Gate on RevenueCat entitlement. Rotate copy every 14 days via PostHog. Suppress on full-screen player/reader/onboarding/paywall screens. Forbid Inter, indigo-500, fake countdowns. Use translucent Liquid Glass material with Cabinet Grotesk + Manrope.'
anti_patterns: 1) Stacking multiple persistent CTAs (top-bar + FAB + tab + inline) = Decision Paralysis. 2) Showing for paying users (entitlement gate forgot). 3) Hardcoded copy never rotated = banner blindness within 14 days. 4) Persistent CTA on full-screen player/reader/onboarding/paywall (interruptive). 5) Fake urgency timer ('Sale ends in 9:59') = DFA Article 25 dark pattern. 6) Confirm-shaming ('Are you sure you want to stay on free?') = DFA + FTC. 7) Persistent CTA pre-paywall in onboarding (breaks AI Plan Reveal peak). 8) Tap routes to dead-end web page instead of in-app paywall.
regulatory_risk: LOW-MEDIUM. Apple Guideline 4.5.4 — push-style upsells outside the app prohibited (persistent CTA is in-app, OK). FTC + DFA — fake urgency badges on persistent CTA = violation. EU DFA Q4 2026 — addictive-design notification frequency may be reviewed if persistent CTA fires push notifications. UK Children's Code — for under-18 audiences, persistent monetization CTAs are flagged.
platform_constraints_2026: Apple: persistent CTA must route to in-app paywall using StoreKit / IAP (Guideline 3.1.1) OR external web checkout via External Purchase Link Entitlement (Guideline 3.1.5(a), post-Epic ruling 2024) with explicit disclosure modal. Apple Guideline 4.5.4 prohibits push-based upsells but in-app persistent CTA is fine. Google Play: persistent in-app CTA must use Play Billing for digital goods; alternative billing via User Choice Billing pilot in some regions.
accessibility_implications: WCAG 2.2 SC 2.5.8 — touch target ≥24x24px (effective 44x44pt iOS, 48x48dp Android). VoiceOver/TalkBack: persistent CTA must announce 'Try Pro, button' (not 'Pro' as ambiguous label). Dynamic Type up to 200% — chip must reflow or wrap, not truncate. Reduce-motion: disable bounce/morph animation on press. SC 1.4.3 contrast 4.5:1 on chip text. SC 2.4.7 focus visible — keyboard nav focus indicator. SC 1.3.1 info and relationships — persistent CTA must NOT be the only way to access upgrade (Settings > Plan must also work — redundant pathway for accessibility).
pitfalls_caveats: 1) Banner blindness within 14 days — copy rotation required. 2) Free-user-only feature: forgetting entitlement gate shows CTA to paying users (annoying, breaks trust). 3) Push-style upsell sent from persistent CTA campaign = Apple 4.5.4 violation. 4) Routing CTA to web checkout requires explicit disclosure modal post-Apple/Epic ruling. 5) Persistent CTA on onboarding screens breaks AI Plan Reveal peak — gate to post-onboarding screens only. 6) Frequency capping for re-engagement push notifications tied to persistent CTA campaigns.
cross_cultural_validity: Persistent CTA pattern translates well globally with locale-appropriate copy. JP / KR: keigo / honorific register on copy ('プロを試す' vs casual 'Try Pro'). DE: Sie register acceptable in B2B; tu/du in consumer wellness. LATAM: aggressive discount-anchoring on rotated copy ('-50%') outperforms outcome-promise. CN: localized social-proof copy + 'verified by App Store'. RTL (AR/HE): mirror chip position to bottom-LEFT (since RTL reads right-to-left).
prescribed_action: Add a persistent Upgrade CTA to all post-onboarding authenticated screens — top-right button (B2B) or bottom-right floating chip (consumer); never both, never stacked. Gate render on RevenueCat / Adapty entitlement (suppress for paying users). Rotate copy every 14 days via PostHog feature flag (3-4 variants minimum). Suppress on full-screen player/reader/onboarding/paywall contexts. Wire tap to paywall navigation with source = 'persistent_cta', placement = current_screen. Telemetry: persistent_cta_viewed / clicked / to_purchase. Expected lift +10-20% revenue (PDF citation 67).
master_prompt_relevance: 06_monetization_compliance (primary), 02_design (chip/button placement + rotation), 04_implementation (entitlement gating + nav wiring), 05_telemetry (impression + CTR + 30d attribution).
questionnaire_relevance: N/A — applies to post-onboarding app shell, not questionnaire screens.
aha_relevance: N/A directly — but persistent CTA captures users who got Aha but skipped first paywall and later hit a feature limit. Indirect Aha-recovery mechanism.
telemetry_events: persistent_cta_viewed (placement, screen, copy_variant), persistent_cta_clicked (placement, screen, copy_variant), persistent_cta_to_paywall_viewed, persistent_cta_to_purchase (with 30d attribution window), persistent_cta_suppressed_for_entitled_user (sanity check).
measurement_horizon: Day 1-30 (CTR per impression), Day 30 (revenue lift vs control without persistent CTA), 12mo cohort LTV (cumulative effect of recovered conversions).
emotional_archetype_alignment: Sage (informed about upgrade affordance), Hero (visible commitment path), Ruler (premium tier exclusivity). MISMATCH: Caregiver (constant upgrade prompts feel coercive — minimize and rotate to gentle copy), Innocent (overt monetization conflicts — use subtle 'Plus features' framing), Outlaw (anti-corporate brand may resent persistent monetization).
anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against: Stacked CTA Decision Paralysis, Banner Blindness (no rotation), Confirm-Shaming Upgrade, Fake Urgency Badge (DFA Article 25), Persistent-During-Onboarding (breaks Aha peak). Aligns with: Hard Paywall Climax (item 34, complementary not redundant), Plan Selection Psychology (item 38), Hybrid Monetization (item 41).
